description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the rust:latest Docker image for most jobs and docker:latest for the build-release and system-test jobs, with a global docker:dind service. Define two stages, build and build-release, and configure each job to use a cache keyed by CI_JOB_NAME that stores the target and .cargo directories (pull policy for audit and format). In the build stage add an audit job that runs apt-get update, apt-get -y --no-install-recommends install libsystemd-dev, cargo install cargo-audit, and cargo audit --ignore RUSTSEC-2020-0071 --ignore RUSTSEC-2020-0159; a build job that runs apt-get update, apt-get -y --no-install-recommends install libsystemd-dev, and cargo build; a clippy job that runs apt-get update, apt-get -y --no-install-recommends install libsystemd-dev, rustup component add clippy, and cargo clippy --release -j 8 -- -D clippy::all; a format job that runs apt-get update, apt-get -y --no-install-recommends install libsystemd-dev, rustup component add rustfmt, and cargo fmt --all -- --check; and a test job that runs apt-get update, apt-get -y --no-install-recommends install libsystemd-dev, ln -sf /usr/share/zoneinfo/Europe/Berlin /etc/localtime, and cargo test. Add a build-release job in the build-release stage that runs only on tags, uses docker:latest, executes export BUILD_FLAGS="--release" and scripts/local/build, caches the same paths, and publishes target/path/cinit as an artifact. Finally, add a system-test job in the build stage that uses docker:latest, runs scripts/local/build, caches the same paths, and also publishes target/path/cinit as an artifact.
